query: What is the most recent NIFC National Fire News year-to-date acres burned figure for 2026, latest available as of late June 2026? Report the exact YTD acres and date.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary:

**The most recent NIFC National Fire News year-to-date acres burned figure for 2026 is 3,168,102 acres** (alongside 36,262 year-to-date wildfires).

- **Exact YTD acres:** 3,168,102
- **Date:** Report dated **July 1, 2026**, with situational data noted "as of **June 29, 2026** at 7:30 a.m. MDT" — the latest business-day update available as of late June 2026.

This figure is confirmed consistently across the NIFC National Fire News page and the NIFC Statistics page, both sourced from the National Interagency Coordination Center's Incident Management Situation Report (IMSR). The report updates Monday–Friday, so the totals reflect activity through roughly June 30, 2026, and are provisional in-season figures subject to revision.

sources.title: Welcome to the Nation's Logistical Support Center | National Interagency Fire Center
sources.snippet: 3 New Large Fires; 51 Large Fires Being Suppressed; 552,639 Acres Burned in Large Fires.

sources.title: Nation's largest wildfire grows to over 70,000 acres in Southern Utah
sources.snippet: The Cottonwood Fire, currently the largest wildfire in the nation, mapped at 70,992 acres and 0% contained.

key_facts: 2026 NIFC year-to-date acres burned = 3,168,102 acres (report dated July 1, 2026; situational data as of June 29, 2026 at 7:30 a.m. MDT).
Identical YTD figures appear on both the NIFC National Fire News page and the Statistics page, confirming cross-source consistency.
The report is updated Monday–Friday and sourced from the Incident Management Situation Report (IMSR) from NICC; YTD reflects activity through roughly June 30, 2026.
As of the July 1, 2026 report: 51 uncontained large fires nationwide, 257 new fires reported the prior day (3 new large fires), 552,639 acres burned on large fires, 9,018 personnel assigned.
Largest active wildfire was the Cottonwood Fire in Southern Utah, mapped at ~70,992 acres and 0% contained (human-caused, ignited late June 2026).
Figures are provisional in-season totals and subject to revision.
